column1: # ViroForge per-collection contamination baselines
# Sample-type default contamination fractions (percent of the library) at the
# 'realistic' level. `viroforge generate --contamination-level` scales these:
# clean 0.25x / realistic 1.0x / heavy 2.5x / failed 4.0x (see
# viroforge/core/contamination.py LEVEL_MULTIPLIERS). Populated into
# body_site_collections by scripts/populate_contamination_defaults.py (folded
# into setup-db). NULL/absent -> global preset fallback.
# These are literature-INFORMED modeling defaults, not per-site empirical
# measurements (the virome literature does not report site-specific VLP-prep host
# fractions). They are set by three well-established drivers:
#   - Host-cell-rich mucosal/tissue samples carry high human DNA (respiratory,
#     skin, vaginal, blood cfDNA); environmental samples are near host-free.
#   - Low-microbial-biomass samples (blood, ocular, urinary, lung) are dominated
#     by reagent/"kitome" contamination (Salter et al. 2014, BMC Biol, PMID 25387460).
#   - Viromes carry quantifiable host + rRNA contamination that QC must detect
#     (Zolfo et al. 2019, ViromeQC, Nat Biotechnol, PMID 31748692). RNA-workflow
#     collections carry a higher ribosomal-RNA baseline (pre-depletion).
# PhiX is a sequencing spike-in (~0.1%), constant and not scaled by level.
# Columns: collection_id, host_organism, host_pct, rrna_pct, reagent_pct, phix_pct, rationale
collection_id
